title: load_entrypoint swallows non-NotFound IO errors and silently picks wrong file
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/memdir/entrypoint.rs:83-106 (load_entrypoint)
## Violation
Non-NotFound IO errors are downgraded to a `warn!` and the loop falls through to the next candidate:

```rust
match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {
    Ok(raw) => { ... return Some(...); }
    Err(err) if err.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {}
    Err(err) => {
        tracing::warn!(path = %path.display(), error = %err, "could not read MEMORY.md candidate — trying next");
    }
}
```

Failure modes this hides:
1. `<cwd>/MEMORY.md` exists but is unreadable (permission denied, EACCES). The function silently loads `~/.openclaudia/MEMORY.md` instead — a different agent persona — and the model gets the wrong project memory with no surfaced error.
2. The file exists but is not valid UTF-8 (binary blob accidentally placed there). Same silent fallback.
3. Disk I/O error mid-read (EIO). Same.

This is the perimeter-defense anti-pattern from §3.4: "Reject invalid inputs and throw immediate exceptions. Do not return default/null values to mask errors." The CLAUDE.md project explicitly mandates `anyhow::Result` + `.context()` for read paths, and `load_entrypoint` is exactly such a perimeter (filesystem → in-process state).
## Mandated refactor
1. Change signature to `pub fn load_entrypoint(cwd: &Path) -> Result<Option<EntrypointFile>>`.
2. Return `Err` on any non-NotFound IO error so the caller (system-prompt builder) can decide whether to abort startup or surface a banner to the user.
3. Specifically distinguish "file exists but unreadable" (Err) from "file does not exist" (continue scanning candidates).
4. Test cases: chmod 000 on the cwd file with a writable home file — must Err, not load the home file.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
